_sources.discrepancies: Nebraska enacted photo ID requirement (LB 514, 2023; effective 2024 general). NCSL confirms Nebraska as requiring photo ID. Voters without ID may cast provisional ballot and return with ID by the second Friday after the election. IMPORTANT: Nebraska's unicameral state legislature (the Legislature) is officially nonpartisan — legislative primaries do not have party lanes. Candidates file without party affiliation; top-two advance to the general. Consequently runoffRules.partyLockedToFirstRoundPrimary is false, not because Nebraska lacks a runoff structure, but because the Legislature's nonpartisan nature means party-lock is inapplicable for those races. Statewide and federal races are partisan with a standard plurality result (no runoffs). Primary date is set by statute as the second Tuesday in May.

earlyVoting.notes: Early voting (in-person) begins 30 days before the primary at county election offices. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Early in-person voting begins 30 days before the general election at county election offices. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Early in-person voting available starting 30 days before the general election at county election commissioner or clerk offices. Mail ballots also available on request. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.

acceptedIds: Nebraska driver's license or state ID card
Student ID from a Nebraska accredited college or university with photo
Nebraska voter ID card (free from county election office)
votingRules.expirationRule: Photo ID must not be expired. Nebraska voter ID cards do not expire. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
votingRules.impedimentDeclaration: Voters without acceptable photo ID may cast a provisional ballot and return with acceptable ID by the second Friday after the election (canvass deadline).
votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Nebraska does not have a statewide prohibition on phones at polling places, but county rules may apply. Voters may not photograph their marked ballot. Check with your county election office.
additionalRules: Closed primary: only voters registered with a party may vote in that party's primary for partisan offices.

IMPORTANT — nonpartisan unicameral legislature: Nebraska's state legislature (the Legislature) is officially nonpartisan and unicameral (one chamber). Legislative primaries do not have party lanes — all candidates run together; the top two advance to the general election regardless of party.
No runoffs in Nebraska — the candidate with the most votes wins in the general election.
Nebraska allocates presidential electoral votes by congressional district (one of only two states to do so, along with Maine).

eligibility: Any registered voter may request an absentee (mail) ballot without providing a reason
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: The Friday before the election (received by county election office) for a mailed ballot; in-person absentee available through the Monday before the election
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by the county election office by the time polls close on Election Day
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Must arrive at the county election office by poll closing on Election Day. Postmarks do not count.
